name: Vandal Kingdom of North Africa
note: The Vandals crossed into North Africa in 429 under Gaiseric, having previously swept through Spain. They captured Carthage in 439, making it their capital. The Vandal fleet dominated the western Mediterranean and sacked Rome in 455. 'Vandalism' as a concept derives from their reputation, though they were no more destructive than other Germanic peoples. Destroyed by the Byzantine general Belisarius in 533 in a remarkably swift campaign.

figures.name: Gaiseric (Geiseric)
figures.significance: Greatest Vandal king; crossed into Africa with 80,000 people (429); built a naval empire; sacked Rome (455) — the second sack in 45 years; the only Germanic king to seriously challenge Roman naval supremacy
